nativeRange.habitat: Reef flats and shallow lagoon reef, typically found singly or in pairs sheltering under ledges or in crevices; depth range 3–50 m.

diet.notes: Feeds primarily on small benthic invertebrates in the wild. In captivity, generally accepts frozen meaty foods such as brine shrimp, mysis shrimp, marine flesh, and calanus. Has been reported to accept quality flake and micro-pellet foods over time. Multiple small daily feedings tend to be preferred over a single large feeding.

avoidWith: large predatory fish that may consume small cardinalfish

summary: A small, peaceful Indo-Pacific cardinalfish with a silver body and bold dark horizontal stripes, generally considered easy to keep and reef-safe. Tends to shelter among rockwork and crevices, with nocturnal feeding habits.
careNotes: The Black Striped Cardinalfish (Ostorhinchus nigrofasciatus, formerly Apogon nigrofasciatus following reclassification) is generally considered an undemanding reef fish well-suited to community marine aquariums. It tends to shelter in and around live rock, caves, and overhangs during the day and is typically more active after lights out. Aquatics Unlimited recommends keeping only one per aquarium, as it is described as less social than some other Apogon relatives; however, mated pairs are commonly kept and the species mouthbroods in pairs. A tank with ample rockwork providing cave-like refuge is strongly advised. Temperature disagreement noted between retail sources (72–78°F / 22–26°C) and FishBase wild-range data (25.4–28.9°C); the range recorded here (23–28°C) represents an averaged consensus, and the dataStatus has been set to needs_review pending further expert review of the lower bound.
breedingNotes: Mouthbrooding has been documented in this species, with distinct pairing during courtship and spawning. Under well-maintained aquarium conditions with a compatible pair, breeding may occur. Exact captive breeding protocols are not well-documented in the hobby literature.

primary.name: LiveAquaria
primary.notes: Listed scientific name as 'Apogon spp.' — cross-referenced with secondary source and FishBase to confirm Ostorhinchus nigrofasciatus.

additional.name: aquaticsunlimited.com
additional.notes: Confirmed scientific name Apogon nigrofasciatus (synonym). Cross-checked sizing (4 in / ~10 cm), temperature, pH, salinity, and tank size. Agreed on 30 gal minimum and peaceful temperament. Recommended keeping only one per tank due to lower social tolerance compared to other cardinalfish species.

additional.name: FishBase
additional.notes: Current accepted name is Ostorhinchus nigrofasciatus (Lachner, 1953). Max size 10 cm TL / 7 cm SL common. Wild temperature range 25.4–28.9°C — higher than retail sources' 72–78°F (22–26°C). Confirmed nocturnal feeder, mouthbrooder, reef-associated habitat. Native range: Mozambique, Red Sea to Tuamoto Islands, north to southern Japan, south to New Caledonia and Rapa.

fish.breedingNotes: Mouthbrooding species. Mated pairs have been documented spawning in captivity under stable aquarium conditions, but raising fry successfully is challenging and not commonly achieved in the hobby.
